summary: Russian forces intensified drone and KAB attacks on multiple Ukrainian settlements, including Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson and Mykolaiv, causing casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukraine reported a fatal incident at the Russian port of Temryuk after a drone strike. Ukrainian air defenses continue to struggle with shortages of munitions against Russian ballistic missiles.
key_points: Russian KAB attacks hit Vasylkivka in Dnipropetrovsk region, injuring nine and damaging a market, shops and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
A drone strike caused a fire and one fatality at the Russian sea port of Temryuk in Krasnodar Krai (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and the Black Sea in one day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Three Shahed drones were reported over Chernihiv and ten over Odesa/Chornomorsk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67587).
Ukrainian forces struck a training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying Russian TOR-M2 and ZU-23 air defence systems (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Russian forces launched about eight KABs on Kherson, with unknown effects (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian officials say a shortage of air defence munitions hampers interception of Russian ballistic missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30883).
New groups of UAVs were detected over the Black Sea heading towards Odesa (Source: @kpszsu/65086).

sections.executive_summary: Russian KAB and drone attacks have escalated across several Ukrainian regions, causing civilian casualties and damage to markets, shops and residential buildings. A Ukrainian drone strike ignited a fire at the Russian port of Temryuk, killing one person. Ukraine's air defence continues to face ammunition shortages, limiting its ability to counter Russian ballistic missiles.

points: In Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk region, Russian KABs injured nine people, set fire to a local market and damaged eight shops, ten trade pavilions and a multi‑storey residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Three Shahed drones were observed over Chernihiv, indicating continued aerial pressure on northern Ukrainian territories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67587).
Ukrainian forces hit a training ground in Zaporizhzhia, destroying Russian TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23 air‑defence systems and inflicting personnel losses (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
In Kherson, Russian forces launched approximately eight KABs; casualties and damage have not yet been confirmed (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Two civilians were injured in Mykolaiv after drone debris fell during a night‑time incident (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
A Ukrainian drone strike caused a fire at the Temryuk sea port in Krasnodar Krai, killing one civilian and wounding three others (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Russian officials claim that Ukrainian attacks targeted infrastructure in the Donbas and Novorossia regions, aiming to reach Russian standards of living by 2030 (Source: @tass_agency/380530).
Russian forces reported a successful interception of a Swedish fighter aircraft attempting to engage a Su‑24 bomber over the Baltic Sea (Source: @tass_agency/380515).
Repeated KAB launches were reported over eastern Dnipropetrovsk and Donetsk regions, though specific damage details were not provided (Source: @kpszsu/65071).
UAV activity was noted in the direction of Poltava from the north‑east, suggesting potential reconnaissance over the eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/65087).
A new reactive UAV from Bryansk was observed heading towards Snovsk/Gorodnya, indicating possible cross‑border incursions (Source: @eRadarrua/96438).
Three UAVs were reported near the town of Ternevka in Dnipropetrovsk region, highlighting continued aerial pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/96439).
Shahed drones were reported over Chernihiv, confirming ongoing aerial threats in the northern sector (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67587).
UAVs were detected moving towards Parafiivka/Ichny from the south‑east in Chernihiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65076).
A UAV was observed near Zhytomyr, flying past Chopyvichi in a south‑west direction (Source: @kpszsu/65064).
Additional UAV activity was reported over Poltava from the north‑east, potentially targeting logistics routes (Source: @kpszsu/65087).
Russian KAB attacks struck a market and residential area in Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk, causing nine injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Two civilians were injured in Mykolaiv after drone debris fell during a night‑time incident (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian forces destroyed Russian air‑defence assets at a training ground in Zaporizhzhia, including TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23 systems (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Ukrainian attacks targeted bridges linking Crimea to the mainland, indicating strategic strikes on southern infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Ukrainian air defence shot down 109 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and the Black Sea in one day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Ukraine reports a shortage of air‑defence munitions, limiting its ability to intercept Russian ballistic missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30883).
Russian forces deployed reconnaissance UAVs over Zaporizhzhia, potentially guiding strike weapons (Source: @kpszsu/65080).
New groups of UAVs were detected over the Black Sea heading towards Odesa, increasing the threat to southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/65086).

key_developments: Russian KAB strikes on Vasylkivka, Dnipropetrovsk injured nine civilians and damaged market, shops and a residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81890).
Ukrainian drone hit Temryuk port in Krasnodar Krai, causing a fire, one death and three injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81889).
Ukrainian forces destroyed Russian TOR‑M2 and ZU‑23 air‑defence systems at a Zaporizhzhia training ground (Source: @UkraineNow/81891).
Shahed drones were reported over Chernihiv, indicating continued aerial threats in the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67587).
Ukrainian air‑defence shot down 109 UAVs over Russian territory and the Black Sea in a single day (Source: @tass_agency/380533).
Ukraine faces a shortage of air‑defence munitions, hampering interception of Russian ballistic missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30883).
New UAV groups detected over the Black Sea heading towards Odesa, raising risk to southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/65086).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV and KAB activity remains high across multiple fronts, targeting civilian infrastructure and logistics hubs, especially in Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia and the southern coastal regions. Ukraine's air‑defence shortages limit its capacity to counter ballistic missile threats, while successful strikes on Russian air‑defence assets show Ukrainian capability to degrade enemy air‑defence. Continued drone incursions over the Black Sea and northern oblasts suggest a sustained Russian effort to pressure supply lines and civilian morale.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian regional authorities have activated emergency services in Vasylkivka and Mykolaiv following KAB and drone impacts, while local administrations in Zaporizhzhia reported rapid repair of destroyed air‑defence systems. Russian emergency responders contained the fire at Temryuk port, but civilian casualties highlight vulnerability of Russian logistics hubs. Both sides appear to be reinforcing air‑defence and civil protection measures in the affected regions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths indicates increased coordination of drone swarms from the Black Sea towards Odesa, requiring heightened radar coverage. The pattern of KAB strikes on market and residential zones suggests a Russian tactic to disrupt civilian supply chains. Ukraine's reported ammunition shortages for air‑defence systems may create exploitable gaps for Russian ballistic missile launches, necessitating prioritisation of resupply channels.
